title: Reading Poetry in English
description: Line breaks, enjambment, meter, voice — reading slowly on purpose.
summary_pt_br: Poesia: quebras de linha, enjambement, métrica, voz. Leia em voz alta, duas vezes.

scenes.subtitle_en: Line breaks, enjambment, meter, voice — reading slowly on purpose.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Poesia: quebras de linha, enjambement, métrica, voz. Leia em voz alta, duas vezes.

scenes.text_en: Line breaks, enjambment, meter, voice — reading slowly on purpose. Focus: reading poetry in english. The line break creates a small silence before the next thought.
scenes.text_pt: Poesia: quebras de linha, enjambement, métrica, voz. Leia em voz alta, duas vezes. Foco: reading poetry in english. A quebra de linha cria um pequeno silêncio.

scenes.title: Reading Poetry in English
scenes.body_en: Line breaks carry meaning. ENJAMBMENT = sentence runs past the line. METER = the rhythm (iambic pentameter etc). VOICE = who speaks, not the poet. Read aloud, twice.
scenes.body_pt: Poesia: quebras de linha, enjambement, métrica, voz. Leia em voz alta, duas vezes.

examples.en: The line break creates a small silence before the next thought.
examples.pt: A quebra de linha cria um pequeno silêncio.

examples.en: Enjambment lets a sentence spill across lines.
examples.pt: Enjambement deixa a frase transbordar as linhas.

examples.en: The speaker is not always the poet.
examples.pt: O falante nem sempre é o poeta.

scenes.title: Key vocabulary — Reading Poetry in English

exercise.question: Complete: "The line break ___ a small silence before the next thought."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "The line break creates a small silence before the next thought."
exercise.hint_pt_br: A quebra de linha cria um pequeno silêncio.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: Enjambment lets a sentence spill across lines.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "Enjambment lets a sentence spill across lines."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Enjambement deixa a frase transbordar as linhas.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Reading Poetry in English — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre reading poetry in english. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Lúcia, how do you begin reading a new poem?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I read it aloud twice, slowly.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What do line breaks do for meaning?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: They create small silences between ideas.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: A sentence that spills past the line.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Is the speaker always the poet?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Rarely — the voice is a constructed self.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What about meter? Why does it matter?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Rhythm carries mood before meaning arrives.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Poetry rewards slowness the world rarely grants.

scenes.title: A poem, out loud
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Analyze the poem's voice, meter, and one striking line. Use at least three poetry terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In the small circle by the window, Dona Lúcia opened a weathered book and read a short poem twice.

paragraphs.speaker: Lúcia
paragraphs.en: Notice how the line break makes you pause — the silence itself belongs to the meaning of that sentence.

paragraphs.speaker: Member
paragraphs.en: And the enjambment in the third stanza — the sentence refuses to end where the line does.

paragraphs.speaker: Lúcia
paragraphs.en: The speaker here is a widow, though we know the poet was thirty and newly in love.

paragraphs.speaker: Member
paragraphs.en: The iambic meter carries her grief like a slow footstep, even before we understand the words.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Poetry, Lúcia said, is the last place where slowness was never considered a flaw.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Reading Poetry in English". Line breaks, enjambment, meter, voice — reading slowly on purpose. The line break creates a small silence before the next thought. Enjambment lets a sentence spill across lines. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Reading Poetry in English". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
check.explanation: She says the title: "Reading Poetry in English".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Reading Poetry in English".

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
